_comment: Enrichment parser. The two computeFields lookups stamp device_*/user_* context on every event. The mappings block then exposes the device identity in OCSF fields so DETECTIONS can bind the Target Asset. ASSET-MAPPING MINIMUM for events-type detection rules: device.uid = the numeric console agent id (device_agentid from the endpoint lookup, NOT the uuid) PLUS an endpoint class_uid (1xxx, e.g. 1007). Binding reconciles device.uid against the live Asset Inventory, so a real enrolled agent id is required. Set {{ENDPOINT_CLASS_UID}} to the source's real OCSF class; it must be an endpoint class (1xxx) for events-rule auto-binding. If this source is NOT endpoint activity (network/identity/cloud), leave class_uid as the source's real class and bind detections via a scheduled rule with entityMappings on the device_host / device_agentid / device_agentuuid columns instead (that path binds for any source). See powerquery/references/detection-rules.md for the tested binding matrix. UNIVERSAL KEY: the unified asset id (device_assetid / user_assetid, the `id` from datasource assets) resolves ANY asset type (device / identity / cloud); stamp it into device.uid (devices) or user.uid (identities) with the matching class_uid. For an identity-centric source, stamp user.uid = user_assetid + an Identity class_uid (3xxx, e.g. 3002) instead of device.uid + 1007.

rewrites.expression: | lookup device_assetid, device_os, device_ip, device_agentid, device_agentuuid, device_lastuser, device_site, device_criticality, device_riskfactors from {{ENDPOINT_TABLE}} by hostname = {{HOSTNAME_FIELD}}
| lookup user_assetid, user_principal, user_sid, user_domain, user_dn, user_groups, user_privileged, user_criticality, user_riskfactors from {{IDENTITY_TABLE}} by username = {{USERNAME_FIELD}}
